question_text: Describe how an electromagnet is used in an electric bell.
correct_answer: When the button is pressed, current flows through the electromagnet, which attracts a metal arm/clapper that hits the bell. When the arm moves, it breaks the circuit, switching off the electromagnet, which releases the arm. The process repeats, creating a ringing sound.
explanation: An electric bell uses an electromagnet that turns on and off repeatedly. This repeated switching makes the clapper hit the bell many times per second, creating the ringing sound we hear.
